change_summary: Fine-Δr ladder blocked: engine blows up at Δr<=0.0032 (t~30-40); KO only delays
metric.name: fine-Δr stable to t>=1500
expected: fine-Δr runs (Δr=0.002-0.0032) stable to t>=1500 for a clean f-mode decay fit (error_fit / continuum)
observed: runs blow up at t≈37/27/4 for Δr=0.0032/0.0024/0.0020; growing oscillation precedes blow-up
root_cause: fine-Δr numerical instability in the nonlinear Cowling BDNK scheme (con2prim/boundary/atmosphere); Kreiss-Oliger σKO=2-6 only delays it
fix_hypothesis: deeper stability fix needed — higher-order/limited reconstruction, refined atmosphere/surface treatment, or implicit-in-source recovery; NOT achievable by KO tuning alone

change_summary: CFL reduction ruled out as fine-Δr stabilizer (worse, not better); confirms spatial-scheme instability
metric.name: CFL=0.1 stable to t>37
expected: reduced CFL (Δt/Δr=0.1) stabilizes the fine-Δr run past t=37
observed: CFL=0.1 blows up EARLIER (t=11.4) than CFL=0.25 (t=37) at Δr=0.0032
root_cause: the fine-Δr blow-up is a SPATIAL-scheme instability (reconstruction / auxiliary-derivative-field evolution dε,dṽ), NOT a CFL/temporal-stability issue — smaller dt (more KO per physical time) makes it worse, confirming dissipation/timestep are not the lever
fix_hypothesis: scheme-level change required: constraint-damping on the evolved derivative fields, a more dissipative/limited surface reconstruction, or reformulating the BDNK auxiliary variables — with the validated coarse-Δr QNM as a regression test. KO (σKO 2-6) and CFL (0.1) both RULED OUT.

change_summary: Localized fine-Δr instability to the origin r≈0 (central spike 3622 vs surface QNM 2e-6) — refines fix to r=0 regularity
metric.name: instability at origin

change_summary: Pinpointed the exact fix target: 1/r source terms at the innermost cell (shum_evolve_opt.jl L474 Srr*(-2/r)+2*Stt/r, L486/490 vbh/r, L473 SrU*2/r). Formally finite at r=0 by odd-parity numerators, but discrete innermost-cell eval amplifies noise by 1/r[1]->inf as Δr->0 (why KO only delays, CFL worsens). Fix: parity-aware l'Hopital regularization of these 1/r terms at i=1 (e.g. f/r -> ∂_r f|_0 via odd-parity ghost), with coarse-Δr QNM F=2.699kHz as regression test. Deferred from this session (delicate multi-term change; risk to validated QNM in deep context) — handed off as a fresh focused effort.
metric.name: fix target pinpointed

change_summary: Origin RHS-override ruled out (delays t~1→20, not cured); only per-term l'Hopital remains; engine reverted clean (validated results intact)
metric.name: override stable to t>=1500
expected: origin RHS override (innermost norig cells set to first clean interior value) cures the fine-Δr instability
observed: override DELAYS blow-up (Δr=0.0024: t~1→16 at norig=3; t~20 at norig=12+σKO=2) with a CLEAN oscillation, but does not cure it — re-grows from cells just outside the override (whack-a-mole)
root_cause: the 1/r amplification affects a radial REGION near the origin, not just the innermost cell; con2prim noise in (Stt-Srr) and vbh is amplified by 1/r across several inner cells
fix_hypothesis: PER-TERM l'Hopital regularization is required: compute (Stt-Srr)/r and vbh/r at inner cells by extrapolating the SMOOTH interior values of those ratios (not dividing noisy numerators by r). KO, CFL, and crude RHS-override all RULED OUT by experiment.
